summary: coral-wraith postinstall script exfiltrates host data and runs shell commands
details: The package's postinstall.js executes automatically on `npm install` and combines system reconnaissance, file reads, shell command execution (curl), and outbound HTTP requests. Specifically, postinstall.js requires `os`, `fs`, `http`, and `child_process`; calls `os.hostname()` and `process.cwd()`; reads files via `fs.readFileSync`; spawns `curl` subprocesses (lines 59–61); and issues HTTP GET/POST traffic via `http.request` (line 83) including to a destination referenced as 127.0.0.1 in the source. The composition (sysinfo collection + filesystem reads + child_process curl + http.request all reachable from a postinstall lifecycle hook) matches an install-time exfiltration / dropper fingerprint. Installing this package on a default `npm install` runs this code without user consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
